projects.title: Patrick Fanella Portfolio
projects.summary: Project-first portfolio platform that pairs a React frontend with a Go API and PostgreSQL-backed seeded content.
projects.description: Built to turn a static portfolio scaffold into a maintainable full-stack product. The work focused on defining API contracts early, wiring a typed React client to DB-backed Go handlers, and creating a repeatable seed/bootstrap path so project content and contact submissions could run against PostgreSQL from day one.
highlights: Defined phase-driven delivery assumptions, API contracts, and launch scope before feature integration.
Connected seeded project content and contact submissions to a PostgreSQL-backed API instead of placeholder in-memory data.
Added async route states and browser smoke coverage to verify the public portfolio journey end to end.

projects.title: MERN Stack Memories App
projects.summary: Authenticated React application for posting memories with titles, images, descriptions, and tags.
projects.description: This project tackled the classic full-stack challenge of keeping authoring, authentication, and media-rich content aligned in one user flow. Signed-in users can create and manage memory posts while the client and API stay coordinated around a simple content model for titles, images, descriptions, and tags.
highlights: Implemented an authenticated CRUD flow for user-generated memory posts.
Structured the product around title, image, description, and tag metadata to keep content entry consistent.
Split responsibilities cleanly across dedicated client and server workspaces in a MERN setup.

projects.title: YelpCamp
projects.summary: Campground and review directory with authentication, image storage, geospatial mapping, and review workflows.
projects.description: Built as a richer multi-surface web application where public browsing, authenticated authoring, and review management all needed to coexist without collapsing into route chaos. The project combines server-rendered pages, map-driven location details, session-based auth, and media uploads to support a more credible public directory experience.
highlights: Supported campground CRUD, reviews, and user accounts inside one cohesive application.
Integrated map-based location workflows and hosted image storage for listing content.
Used session and cookie handling to protect authoring paths and review actions.

projects.title: Unique ID Rotating Logger
projects.summary: Node logging utility that writes access logs, rotates them on schedule, compresses archives, and keeps retention bounded.
projects.description: A focused backend utility aimed at a common operational gap: request logs are useful only if they stay traceable and do not grow forever. The logger pairs unique request identifiers with rotating-file retention so lightweight Express services can preserve useful signal without letting local log storage sprawl out of control.
highlights: Generated unique request-level log identifiers for easier tracing during local debugging.
Compressed rotated log files and pruned older archives automatically.
Kept the implementation small enough to drop into lightweight Express services without extra infrastructure.
